description_long: This gatehouse is a historic stone structure featuring a steeply pitched gable roof and elaborate Gothic architectural detailing. The main section includes a large arched doorway with ornate carvings and decorative stonework, flanked by pilasters and crowned with a finial. Adjacent to the main building is a separate arched gateway, also constructed from weathered stone blocks, with a keystone and relief sculpture above the arch. The facade is adorned with intricate tracery, niches, and sculptural elements, giving it a grand and imposing appearance. The stonework is aged, with visible discoloration and moss at the base, suggesting centuries of exposure to the elements. There are few, if any, windows, and the structure appears solid and fortified, typical of medieval or Gothic gatehouses.
description: A grand, weathered stone gatehouse with a steep gable roof, ornate Gothic detailing, and an adjacent arched gateway, exuding historic and fortified character.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main gabled building with its ornate arched doorway and decorative stonework, alongside the adjacent arched gateway.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the thinness of the structure and the depth of the stone walls, with little detail visible except the edge of the roof and arch.
description_view_2: The rear angle highlights the symmetry between the main building and the arched gateway, with the gable and arch both clearly visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile again emphasizes the narrowness of the structure, with the stonework edge and base visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Large arched stone doorway with ornate carvings and a decorative keystone.
attribute_window_placement: Few or no windows, with possible small openings near the roofline.
